questions.answer:

在生活用车的市场覆盖方面，路特斯科技的生活方式车型已进入**多个国家和地区**，尽管具体数量未明确提及，但其在欧洲、中国、北美及其他地区的交付量显著增长，展现了强劲的全球市场扩展能力。这表明路特斯科技的市场覆盖范围广泛，并持续致力于国际化发展。

未来，随着更多信息的更新，路特斯科技在全球门店数量及市场覆盖方面的具体数据将进一步明晰。

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology recorded revenue of \$679 million in 2023, driven by growing sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company achieved a gross profit margin of 15% in the first year of BEV deliveries, with the scaling of asset-light production. Lotus Technology expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, with new flagship locations in Paris, London, and Seoul. The company expects to achieve about a threefold increase in deliveries in 2024 amid steady market expansion. New York - April 8, 2024 - Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Tech" or "the Company"), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and the year ended December 31, 2023. The results reflect steady progress in Lotus Technology's execution of its Vision80 strategy to become an all-electric, intelligent, and luxury mobility provider before Lotus Technology's 80th anniversary in 2028. In its first year of battery electric vehicle (BEV) delivery, Lotus Technology achieved revenue of \$679 million and a gross profit margin of 15% in 2023. Revenue in the fourth quarter of 2023 was \$361 million, representing a 92% quarter-on-quarter increase. This strong performance was enabled by Lotus Technology's asset-light business model, as the company ramped up production and sales of BEV models. Lotus Technology delivered 6,970 vehicles in 2023, an annual record in the 76-year history of the Lotus brand, with battery electric vehicle (BEV) models representing 63% of total deliveries. Vehicle deliveries in the fourth quarter of 2023 increased nearly 110% quarter-over-quarter to 3,749.

In 2024, the company expects to increase its total deliveries about threefold to 26,000 and further grow its healthy gross margin to 17-19%. – May 29, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Technology" or the "Company") (Nasdaq: LOT), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2024. Lotus Technology achieved total revenue of \$173 million in the quarter, a year-on-year growth of 811% and a gross profit margin of 18%, powered by an asset-light model and high-margin businesses including research and development services and accessories. Lotus Technology successfully launched its Chapman Bespoke service in the first quarter of 2024, providing premium customization to Lotus vehicles. Lotus Technology delivered a total of 2,194 vehicles in the first quarter. The Company began deliveries of the Emeya in China, its first electric hyper-grand tourer, and further expanded its product portfolio to four models. In the China market where the Company operates a direct-to-customer sales model, the deliveries in the first quarter had a year-on-year growth of over 100%. Customer deliveries of the Emira, the brand’s internal combustion engine sports car manufactured in the UK, also commenced in the US at the beginning of this year. Global markets outside China contributed over 75% of Lotus Technology's deliveries in the first quarter.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Business 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Our Business**

Lotus is a premier global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions, renowned for designing and developing luxury lifestyle vehicles under its iconic British brand. Established in the U.K. in 1948, Lotus has a rich heritage of over seven decades in racing, symbolizing excellence in performance, design, and engineering, with a legacy that includes 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles.

In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK, transforming the company into a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer. This strategic partnership enhances Lotus's capabilities in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development (R&D), and logistics, facilitating a transition from a traditional sports car company to a leader in high-performance electric vehicles. The luxury BEV market is currently underserved, with only about 10 existing models compared to over 100 internal combustion engine luxury models, positioning Lotus to capitalize on first-mover advantages.

Lotus launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, the Eletre, in 2022, with deliveries commencing in 2023. The Eletre features an advanced 800-volt EPA platform, offering three versions: Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R. The Eletre R achieves an impressive 905 horsepower and accelerates from 0-100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. Its 112-kWh battery provides a WLTP range of 490 km and can be charged from 10% to 80% in under 20 minutes, with an average MSRP exceeding $100,000. Following the Eletre, the second model, the Emeya, was launched in September 2023, with deliveries expected to begin in March 2024.

Lotus's R&D capabilities are a competitive strength, highlighted by the proprietary 800-volt EPA developed over five years, which supports rapid model rollout and production scalability. The company also features advanced ADAS technology, including the first deployable LiDAR system. R&D facilities in Europe and China foster collaboration among experienced teams, enhancing innovation.

All BEV lifestyle models are manufactured through a contract partnership with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art EV manufacturing facilities in Wuhan, China. This partnership enhances scalability and flexibility while minimizing upfront capital commitments. Access to Geely’s global supply chain allows Lotus to secure high-quality components at competitive prices, effectively managing supply chain risks.

Lotus's sales strategy emphasizes a digital-first, omni-channel approach, providing a luxury retail experience through premium stores and a comprehensive digital platform. As of June 30, 2024, the company has established a global distribution network with 207 stores and a Global Commercial Platform for distributing Lotus vehicles, including the Eletre and Emeya. In 2023, Lotus delivered 6,970 vehicles, with 4,873 vehicles delivered in the first half of 2024, encompassing both lifestyle and sports cars.

This comprehensive approach positions Lotus as a leader in the luxury BEV market, leveraging its rich heritage, innovative technology, and strategic partnerships to drive future growth.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology’s American Depositary Shares will begin trading on the Nasdaq on February 23, 2024, under the ticker symbol “LOT.” New York and Singapore - February 20, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, and L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (“L CAA”) (NASDAQ: LCAA), a special purpose acquisition company formed by affiliates of L Catterton, a leading global consumer-focused investment firm, announced today that their previously proposed business combination is expected to be completed on February 22, 2024. The combined company will retain Lotus Technology Inc.'s name as “Lotus Technology Inc.” and its American Depositary Shares (ADS) will commence trading on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol “LOT” on February 23, 2024. The business combination was approved by L CAA shareholders at an extraordinary general meeting held on February 2, 2024. Integrating Lotus Technology’s sports car DNA with a new generation of premium lifestyle vehicles, Lotus Technology Inc. is committed to transforming the brand into an advanced, fully electric, intelligent, and sustainable luxury mobility provider before its 80th anniversary in 2028. Lotus Technology Inc. has already set this ambitious plan in motion by launching a leading product portfolio of high-performing battery electric vehicles (“BEVs”) ahead of other global luxury automakers. These BEVs include Eletre, a new breed of all-electric hyper-SUV powered by Lotus Technology’s proprietary 800-volt EPA, and Emeya, an all-electric hyper-GT among the most advanced vehicles of its kind in the world.

Lotus Technology recorded revenue of \$173 million and a gross profit margin of 18%. The company delivered approximately 2,194 vehicles, with global markets outside China contributing over 75% of total deliveries. Lotus Technology revealed luxury customized editions of classic sports cars and battery electric vehicle lifestyle models. New York  
– May 29, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Technology" or the "Company") (Nasdaq: LOT), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2024. Lotus Technology achieved total revenue of \$173 million in the quarter, a year-on-year growth of 811% and a gross profit margin of 18%, powered by an asset-light model and high-margin businesses including research and development services and accessories. Lotus Technology successfully launched its Chapman Bespoke service in the first quarter of 2024, providing premium customization to Lotus vehicles. Lotus Technology delivered a total of 2,194 vehicles in the first quarter. The Company began deliveries of the Emeya in China, its first electric hyper-grand tourer, and further expanded its product portfolio to four models. In the China market where the Company operates a direct-to-customer sales model, the deliveries in the first quarter had a year-on-year growth of over 100%. Customer deliveries of the Emira, the brand’s internal combustion engine sports car manufactured in the UK, also commenced in the US at the beginning of this year. Global markets outside China contributed over 75% of Lotus Technology's deliveries in the first quarter.

summary: Lotus Technology Inc. is a foreign company that files a FORM 6-K with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to provide updates on significant corporate events and financial performance. This report is essential for ensuring that U.S. investors have access to timely information about the company's operations, particularly as it pertains to its financial health and corporate governance.

For the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology reported substantial growth in key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. Deliveries increased by 239%, reaching 4,873 units, while revenues surged to $398 million, a 206% rise. Despite these gains, the company faced rising costs, leading to an operating loss of $438 million and a net loss of $460 million for the first half of 2024. The adjusted net loss also increased, reflecting ongoing financial challenges.

The company's balance sheet as of June 30, 2024, showed total assets rising significantly to $2.5 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. However, total liabilities also grew, resulting in a shareholders' deficit that improved but remained negative. Notably, the company eliminated mezzanine equity, indicating a shift in its capital structure.

Lotus Technology's product lineup includes several high-performance vehicle models, with projections indicating continued growth and profitability by 2026. The company anticipates producing over 30,000 units and achieving a gross profit margin exceeding 20%. Recent product launches, including limited-edition models, highlight the brand's focus on luxury and exclusivity.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates significant revenue growth and operational advancements, it continues to navigate challenges related to rising costs and net losses. The financial data presented underscores both the company's potential and the need for careful management of its financial resources as it aims for future profitability.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Overview 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Lotus Overview**

Lotus, established in the U.K. in 1948, is a premier global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility, specializing in the design and development of luxury lifestyle vehicles under its iconic brand. With a rich racing heritage, Lotus has secured 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles, enhancing its reputation in the automotive industry. In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK, facilitating its transition from a traditional sports car manufacturer to a leader in high-performance battery electric vehicles (BEVs). Geely's support in manufacturing, research and development (R&D), and supply chain management has been instrumental in this transformation.

The global luxury BEV market, defined as vehicles with a Manufacturer's Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) exceeding $80,000, is expected to experience significant growth but remains underserved, with only about 10 luxury BEV models currently available compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) luxury models. As an early mover in this space, Lotus launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, the Eletre, in 2022. The Eletre features an advanced 800-volt EPA platform, delivering exceptional performance with three available versions, including the Eletre R, which boasts 905 horsepower and accelerates from 0 to 100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. Following this, the second model, Emeya, was launched in September 2023, with deliveries scheduled to commence in March 2024.

Leveraging its storied sports car design heritage, Lotus possesses robust R&D capabilities that provide a competitive advantage, enabling rapid model rollout and scalable production. All BEV lifestyle models are manufactured through a contract with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art facilities in Wuhan, China. This partnership enhances scalability and flexibility while minimizing upfront capital commitments, and it also ensures access to high-quality components through established supplier relationships.

To enhance customer experience, Lotus employs a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, operating 207 stores globally as of June 30, 2024, with plans for further expansion. The company’s global distribution network is supported by a Distribution Agreement with Lotus UK, allowing for efficient marketing and distribution of Lotus vehicles, including the Eletre and Emeya. In terms of sales performance, Lotus delivered 6,970 vehicles in 2023 and 4,873 vehicles in the first half of 2024, encompassing both lifestyle and sports car models.

In summary, Lotus is strategically positioned in the growing luxury BEV market, backed by Geely's resources and expertise, with a strong focus on innovation, customer experience, and operational efficiency.

As of June 30, 2024, Lotus Technology had 207 stores in its global distribution network. Major vehicle models under the Distribution Agreement include Eletre, Emeya, and future lifestyle models, as well as certain sports car models that were or will be developed and manufactured by Lotus UK, such as Evija (BEV sports car) and Emira (ICE sports car).

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Business 
summary: We are a pioneering luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer under the esteemed British brand "Lotus," which has over 70 years of racing heritage and is renowned for its performance, design, and engineering excellence. With the support of Geely Holding, which acquired a 51% stake in Lotus in 2017, we are transitioning from traditional sports cars to high-performance electric vehicles, leveraging cutting-edge technology and a robust research and development framework. 

The global luxury BEV market, projected to grow significantly, is currently underserved, with only a handful of luxury BEV models available compared to a multitude of internal combustion engine options. As a leader in this sector, we aim to launch our E-segment BEV model and achieve a 100% BEV product portfolio by 2027. Our first fully electric model, the Hyper-SUV Eletre, debuted in 2022, showcasing advanced technology and exceptional performance, with plans for further electric vehicle introductions in the coming years.

Our proprietary 800-volt Electric Power Architecture enables rapid model introductions and efficient production, while our Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS) incorporates cutting-edge features like deployable LiDAR. Manufacturing is conducted in partnership with Geely at their advanced facilities in Wuhan, China, enhancing scalability and supply chain management.

We prioritize a luxury retail experience through a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, operating premium stores and a global digital platform that enables vehicle customization and direct customer engagement. With a growing global distribution network and plans to expand our store presence, we delivered 6,970 vehicles in 2023, including both lifestyle and sports cars, and are poised for continued growth in the luxury BEV market.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Overview 
summary: We are a leading global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions, specializing in the design and development of luxury lifestyle vehicles under the esteemed British brand "Lotus." With a rich racing heritage spanning over 70 years, Lotus is renowned for its high-performance standards in both design and engineering. Since Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK in 2017, we have successfully transitioned into a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer, benefiting from Geely's support across various operational areas.

Our flagship model, the Eletre, is a luxury E-segment SUV that showcases our innovative 800-volt Electrical Performance Architecture (EPA). The Eletre is available in three versions: Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R. The Eletre R stands out with an impressive 905 horsepower, achieving acceleration from 0 to 100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds, and offers a WLTP range of 490 km, complemented by rapid charging capabilities. Deliveries of the Eletre commenced in 2023, and we launched our second model, the Emeya, in September 2023, with deliveries set to begin in March 2024. Furthermore, we have plans to introduce two additional models in the near future.

Our research and development capabilities, deeply rooted in Lotus's sports car legacy, serve as a significant competitive advantage, exemplified by the five-year development of the EPA, which facilitates rapid model rollout and enhances production efficiency. Additionally, we have developed an advanced Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS) featuring Level 4-ready hardware and a deployable LiDAR system.

To enhance customer experience, we employ a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, operating premium stores in high-traffic areas alongside a global digital platform for vehicle customization and purchasing. As of June 30, 2024, our global distribution network comprises 207 stores. In 2023, we delivered a total of 6,970 vehicles, with 4,873 vehicles delivered in the first half of 2024. Our workforce consists of 2,989 full-time employees, with a substantial 54.6% dedicated to research and development. 

This comprehensive approach positions us at the forefront of the luxury electric vehicle market, driving innovation and excellence in mobility solutions.

title: 2024-AYEAROFGROWTH 
summary: Lotus Cars is undergoing a strategic transformation to become an intelligent luxury mobility provider, emphasizing the development and integration of Battery Electric Vehicle (BEV) models. This shift aims to enhance sales volume and profit margins while positioning the company as a leader in the luxury automotive market. By focusing on advanced technologies and sustainable practices, Lotus is set to attract a growing customer base interested in eco-friendly luxury options, which is expected to drive significant growth and profitability.

In 2024, the company plans to triple its delivery volume and strengthen client relationships by introducing four new delivery models. Key initiatives will include optimizing operational efficiency, reducing costs, and implementing best practices to streamline operations. Additionally, targeted marketing, improved customer service, and a refined product assortment will be prioritized to maximize revenue per location, with continuous assessment and adaptation being crucial to achieving these goals.

Performance metrics from 2018 to 2024 indicate a remarkable growth trajectory for Lotus Cars. Sales volume increased from an average of 1,200 units annually (2018-2022) to 6,970 units in 2023, with projections for 2024 reaching 26,000 units. Revenue followed a similar trend, jumping from an average of $100 million during 2018-2022 to $679 million in 2023, with estimates for 2024 ranging between $2,500 million and $2,700 million. The gross profit margin also improved significantly, rising from an average of 3% (2018-2022) to 15% in 2023, with projections for 2024 suggesting further growth to between 17% and 19%. 

Overall, these metrics highlight Lotus Cars' ambitious trajectory towards becoming a leader in the luxury electric vehicle market, with a strong focus on growth in production, revenue, and profitability.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Overview 
summary: We are a leading global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions, specializing in the design and development of luxury lifestyle vehicles under the esteemed British brand "Lotus." With a rich racing heritage spanning over 70 years, Lotus is renowned for its high-performance standards in both design and engineering. Since Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK in 2017, we have successfully transitioned into a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer, benefiting from Geely's support across various operational areas.

Our flagship model, the Eletre, is a luxury E-segment SUV that showcases our innovative 800-volt Electrical Performance Architecture (EPA). The Eletre is available in three versions: Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R. The Eletre R stands out with an impressive 905 horsepower, achieving acceleration from 0 to 100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds, and offers a WLTP range of 490 km, complemented by rapid charging capabilities. Deliveries of the Eletre commenced in 2023, and we launched our second model, the Emeya, in September 2023, with deliveries set to begin in March 2024. Furthermore, we have plans to introduce two additional models in the near future.

Our research and development capabilities, deeply rooted in Lotus's sports car legacy, serve as a significant competitive advantage, exemplified by the five-year development of the EPA, which facilitates rapid model rollout and enhances production efficiency. Additionally, we have developed an advanced Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS) featuring Level 4-ready hardware and a deployable LiDAR system.

To enhance customer experience, we employ a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, operating premium stores in high-traffic areas alongside a global digital platform for vehicle customization and purchasing. As of June 30, 2024, our global distribution network comprises 207 stores. In 2023, we delivered a total of 6,970 vehicles, with 4,873 vehicles delivered in the first half of 2024. Our workforce consists of 2,989 full-time employees, with a substantial 54.6% dedicated to research and development. 

This comprehensive approach positions us at the forefront of the luxury electric vehicle market, driving innovation and excellence in mobility solutions.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Overview 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Lotus Overview**

Lotus, established in the U.K. in 1948, is a premier global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility, specializing in the design and development of luxury lifestyle vehicles under its iconic brand. With a rich racing heritage, Lotus has secured 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles, enhancing its reputation in the automotive industry. In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK, facilitating its transition from a traditional sports car manufacturer to a leader in high-performance battery electric vehicles (BEVs). Geely's support in manufacturing, research and development (R&D), and supply chain management has been instrumental in this transformation.

The global luxury BEV market, defined as vehicles with a Manufacturer's Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) exceeding $80,000, is expected to experience significant growth but remains underserved, with only about 10 luxury BEV models currently available compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) luxury models. As an early mover in this space, Lotus launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, the Eletre, in 2022. The Eletre features an advanced 800-volt EPA platform, delivering exceptional performance with three available versions, including the Eletre R, which boasts 905 horsepower and accelerates from 0 to 100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. Following this, the second model, Emeya, was launched in September 2023, with deliveries scheduled to commence in March 2024.

Leveraging its storied sports car design heritage, Lotus possesses robust R&D capabilities that provide a competitive advantage, enabling rapid model rollout and scalable production. All BEV lifestyle models are manufactured through a contract with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art facilities in Wuhan, China. This partnership enhances scalability and flexibility while minimizing upfront capital commitments, and it also ensures access to high-quality components through established supplier relationships.

To enhance customer experience, Lotus employs a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, operating 207 stores globally as of June 30, 2024, with plans for further expansion. The company’s global distribution network is supported by a Distribution Agreement with Lotus UK, allowing for efficient marketing and distribution of Lotus vehicles, including the Eletre and Emeya. In terms of sales performance, Lotus delivered 6,970 vehicles in 2023 and 4,873 vehicles in the first half of 2024, encompassing both lifestyle and sports car models.

In summary, Lotus is strategically positioned in the growing luxury BEV market, backed by Geely's resources and expertise, with a strong focus on innovation, customer experience, and operational efficiency.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Operational and Financial Summaries 
summary: The operational results for Lotus Tech for the first quarter of 2024 (1Q 2024) reveal substantial growth across various performance metrics compared to the same period in 2023 (1Q 2023). Key highlights include a dramatic increase in vehicle deliveries, with total units reaching 2,194, marking a 731% rise from 264 units in 1Q 2023. Notably, lifestyle vehicle deliveries surged to 1,047 units, up 344% from 236 units, while sportscar deliveries rose to 1,147 units from just 28 units, reflecting strong demand and customer preferences.

Financially, Lotus Tech reported revenues of 173 million in 1Q 2024, an impressive 811% increase from 19 million in the previous year, with total revenue for FY2023 at 679 million. However, the cost of revenues also escalated to 143 million, a 653% increase, leading to a gross profit of 30 million and a gross margin of 18%. Despite these positive trends, the company faced challenges with an operating loss of 233 million, a 44% increase from the prior year, and a net loss of 258 million, up 61%. Adjusted EBITDA also declined to -204 million.

Overall, while Lotus Tech has demonstrated significant growth in deliveries and revenue, it continues to experience substantial operating and net losses as it advances its Vision80 plan for international expansion and innovation. The performance indicators underscore both the potential and the challenges the organization faces in a competitive market.

title: Deliveries (1)  by Model Type 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Vehicle Deliveries by Model Type and Region for first half 2024**

In the first half of 2024, Lotus Tech has reported remarkable growth in vehicle deliveries, achieving a total of 4,873 units, which marks a 239% increase from 1,439 units delivered in the same period of 2023. This growth is particularly notable across various model types and regions, despite facing potential regulatory challenges in China that could impact operations and securities offerings.

**Key Deliveries Data:**
- **Model Types:**
  - **Lifestyle SUVs and Sedans:** 2,389 units delivered, representing a significant 174% increase from 871 units in first half 2023.
  - **Sportscars:** 2,484 units delivered, reflecting a staggering 337% increase from 568 units in first half 2023.
  - **Total Deliveries:** 4,873 units, up from 1,439 units in first half 2023.

- **Regional Performance:**
  - **Europe:** 1,459 units delivered, accounting for 30% of total deliveries, a substantial rise from 89 units in first half 2023.
  - **China:** 1,208 units delivered, making up 25% of total deliveries, an increase from 965 units in first half 2023.
  - **North America:** 1,278 units delivered, representing 26% of total deliveries, with no prior year comparison available.
  - **Rest of the World:** 928 units delivered, contributing 19% to total deliveries, up from 385 units in first half 2023.

**Additional Insights:**
Lotus Tech has expanded its market reach by commencing deliveries of the Eletre to new regions, including the Gulf Cooperation Council, New Zealand, India, and Egypt, while also initiating production orders for Australia, Japan, and Korea. The Emeya has started deliveries in Europe, with ongoing reservations in the Gulf Cooperation Council, Japan, Korea, and Southeast Asia.

The average selling price of vehicles exceeded US$100,000 in the first half of 2024, despite increased competition and rapid growth in the market. In light of evolving market conditions and new tariff policies in the U.S. and EU, Lotus Tech has revised its 2024 delivery target to 12,000 units.

To ensure sustainable growth and profitability, the company has launched the “Win26” plan, aiming for positive EBITDA and operating cash flow by 2026. This plan focuses on process optimization, cost control, and recalibrating the product plan to cater to diversified markets.

Overall, Lotus Tech's performance in first half 2024 showcases its resilience and strategic initiatives to navigate regulatory challenges while capitalizing on market opportunities.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Substantial uncertainties exist with respect to the interpretation and implementation of newly enacted 2019 PRC Foreign Investment Law and its Implementation Rules. 
summary: On March 15, 2019, China’s National People's Congress enacted the 2019 Foreign Investment Law, effective January 1, 2020, replacing previous regulations and aiming to align foreign investment practices with international standards. This law introduces a "negative list" of restricted industries, creating uncertainty for businesses regarding future restrictions and the potential need for additional market entry clearances. The law also imposes strict regulations on loans and capital contributions from offshore holding companies to their PRC subsidiaries, which could significantly impact liquidity and operational growth.

The regulatory environment in China is complex, with ongoing changes and a lack of clarity that can lead to unintentional violations. Companies must navigate stringent labor laws, social insurance requirements, and foreign exchange regulations, which can restrict their ability to manage finances and operations effectively. Fluctuations in the Renminbi (RMB) and government controls over currency conversion further complicate financial management, potentially affecting revenues and the ability to distribute dividends.

Additionally, compliance with the State Administration of Foreign Exchange (SAFE) regulations is critical for PRC residents and entities involved in offshore investments. Failure to register with SAFE can hinder profit distributions and expose the company to legal liabilities. The evolving regulatory landscape, rising labor costs, and challenges in ensuring compliance pose significant risks to business operations and financial performance in China. Overall, the uncertainties in the legal framework and regulatory environment could adversely affect the company's governance, operational capabilities, and growth potential.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 5. How is the company's overseas expansion situation?
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Lotus's Overseas Expansion Situation**

Lotus, a prestigious automotive brand with a rich 76-year history, has established a formidable global presence, operating over 200 sales outlets worldwide. The company has achieved a well-balanced global delivery distribution, with Europe, China, North America, and the Rest of World each contributing 20-30% to its total deliveries. 

As part of its strategic expansion, Lotus is enhancing its lifestyle vehicle offerings, notably with the introduction of the Eletre SUV and the Emeya GT sedan. The Eletre has commenced deliveries in Southeast Asia and is currently being rolled out in the Middle East, New Zealand, India, and Egypt. Additionally, production orders for the Eletre have begun in Australia, Japan, and South Korea, indicating a robust pipeline for future deliveries.

Meanwhile, the Emeya GT sedan has initiated its delivery phase in Europe and has garnered pre-orders from regions including the Middle East, Northeast Asia, and Southeast Asia. This expansion into diverse international markets underscores Lotus's commitment to broadening its global footprint and catering to the evolving demands of consumers worldwide.

In summary, Lotus's overseas expansion strategy is characterized by a balanced global distribution network, the introduction of new vehicle models, and a proactive approach to entering emerging markets, positioning the brand for sustained growth in the competitive automotive landscape.

Additionally, pursuant to the Distribution Agreement, existing stores and dealers of Lotus UK are transferred to Lotus Technology. As of June 30, 2024, Lotus Technology had 207 stores in its global distribution network. Major vehicle models under the Distribution Agreement include Eletre, Emeya, and future lifestyle models, as well as certain sports car models that were or will be developed and manufactured by Lotus UK, such as Evija (BEV sports car) and Emira (ICE sports car).

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Global Commercial Platform 
summary: The Global Commercial Platform for Lotus UK vehicles, as of June 30, 2024, encompasses a network of 207 stores designed to provide a luxurious and future-proof customer experience through a digital-first, omni-channel sales model. This platform supports key vehicle models such as the Eletre and Emeya, featuring a range of innovative digital tools including a virtual showroom, an inquiry and customization platform, a reservation system for test drives, and comprehensive aftersales services. Customers are offered extensive customization options alongside a digital payment system to enhance their purchasing experience.

Under a Master Distribution Agreement with Lotus Cars Limited, Lotus Tech International Limited (LTIL) serves as the exclusive global distributor for Lotus vehicles, with the exception of the U.S. market, where LTIL acts as the head distributor. This agreement outlines annual business plans and targets that are informed by historical sales data, demand forecasts, and production capacity. As part of this arrangement, existing Lotus UK stores and dealers have been transitioned to Lotus Tech.

The major vehicle models covered under this distribution agreement include the Eletre, Emeya, and future lifestyle models, in addition to high-performance sports cars such as the Evija (Battery Electric Vehicle) and Emira (Internal Combustion Engine). This strategic framework aims to streamline the sales and distribution process while enhancing customer engagement and satisfaction in the luxury automotive market.
